dsl_rule.translation_notes: Translated z-score conditions using Bollinger Bands percentile (bb_pct_b). Entry occurs when price deviates beyond 1 standard deviation (bb_pct_b < 0.0 for long, > 1.0 for short). Exit when price returns to moving average (bb_pct_b ≈ 0.5 or close crosses sma_20). Used sma_20 as the moving average reference point for mean reversion. bb_pct_b directly represents normalized distance from moving average in standard deviations.

backtest_rule.reason: Translated z-score conditions using Bollinger Bands percentile (bb_pct_b). Entry occurs when price deviates beyond 1 standard deviation (bb_pct_b < 0.0 for long, > 1.0 for short). Exit when price returns to moving average (bb_pct_b ≈ 0.5 or close crosses sma_20). Used sma_20 as the moving average reference point for mean reversion. bb_pct_b directly represents normalized distance from moving average in standard deviations.

source_rule_text: A mean-reversion strategy using Bollinger Bands that trades when price deviates from its moving average by more than one standard deviation, with separate entry and exit thresholds.
